name: Opulent Chess
author: Greg Strong (2005) / AGP port
description: Greg Strong's 10x10 Grand Chess derivative (2005) with 10 piece types. Alongside the Chancellor (rook+knight) and Archbishop (bishop+knight) it adds two short-range leapers — the Wizard (camel + one step diagonally, colorbound) and the Lion (Betza's Half-Duck: leaps 2 or 3 squares orthogonally over anything, or steps one diagonally) — and strengthens the Knight with an extra one-square orthogonal step, so Bishop, Knight, Lion and Wizard are all worth about the same. Pawns start on the third rank (double step, en passant); no castling; Grand-Chess promotion: only to a piece type you have lost — optional on ranks 8/9, compulsory on rank 10 (with nothing to promote to, a pawn may not enter the last rank). Checkmate wins.
